snapshots_overview.title: Snapshots - Your Audio Time Machine
snapshots_overview.content:
Snapshots let you save your current setup and restore it later. It's like a 'save game' for your audio configuration!

Perfect for:
• Switching between different setups (work, music, gaming)
• Experimenting without fear
• Quickly restoring a known-good configuration
snapshots_overview.tip: Press S to open the Snapshots panel. Save often, restore confidently!

what_gets_saved.title: What Gets Saved?
what_gets_saved.content:
A snapshot captures:

🔗 Connections - Which nodes are wired to which
🔊 Volumes - All volume levels and mute states
📍 Positions - Where nodes are placed on the graph

Basically, everything you'd need to recreate your exact setup!
what_gets_saved.tip: Snapshots are stored locally - they persist even after closing Pipeflow.

creating_snapshots.title: Creating a Snapshot
creating_snapshots.content:
To save your current setup:

1. Open the Snapshots panel (S)
2. Click 'Save Snapshot'
3. Give it a descriptive name

The snapshot is saved immediately with the current timestamp.
creating_snapshots.tip: Use descriptive names like 'Streaming Setup v2' or 'Recording Session 2024-01' - future you will thank you!

restoring_snapshots.title: Restoring a Snapshot
restoring_snapshots.content:
To restore a saved setup:

1. Open the Snapshots panel (S)
2. Find the snapshot you want
3. Click 'Restore'

Pipeflow will recreate the connections and settings as best it can.

Note: If some nodes no longer exist (apps closed, devices unplugged), those connections will be skipped.
restoring_snapshots.tip: Restoring only affects nodes that exist - it won't launch applications for you.

partial_restore.title: Smart Matching
partial_restore.content:
When you restore, Pipeflow intelligently matches nodes:

• It looks for nodes with the same name and type
• If an exact match isn't found, it tries similar matches
• Unmatched connections are skipped (no errors!)

This means snapshots work even when your system changes a bit.
partial_restore.tip: Node IDs change when apps restart - that's why Pipeflow matches by name and type instead.

snapshot_management.title: Managing Snapshots
snapshot_management.content:
In the Snapshots panel you can:

• See all saved snapshots with timestamps
• Preview what each snapshot contains (connections, volumes)
• Restore any snapshot
• Delete snapshots you no longer need

Snapshots don't take much space, so keep as many as you like!
snapshot_management.tip: You can create multiple snapshots for the same basic setup - one for each tweak you might want.
